text: I today's episode we'll take a dive into the seafood sector.
Is it time to prepare for more favorable markets for the salmon farmers?
Or could COVID-19 hurt demand longer than expected?
And how is movie, the world's largest producer of Atlantic salmon positioning themselves for the future?
Welcome to Utbyta, the D&B podcast where we explain the financial markets and the global economy.
My name is Marius Brunhagen from the D&B markets.
And today I'm joined by Ivan Vinnheim, the CEO of Movie.
Welcome Ivan, how are you?
I'm fine, thank you. Thanks for asking. Good morning Marius.
Yes, we are also joined by my colleague Alexander Oxner, the Seafood Analyst here at the D&B markets.
Welcome Alex.
Thank you, thank you for having me.
I'll start with you Alex, because you have a positive view on the seafood sector.
What are the main reasons investors should take a look at the sector now?
I think the easy explanation is the salmon price.
We expect the salmon price to continue to rise from the current depressed levels, particularly in 2021.
Towards the second half of 2021, I do believe volume growth globally is going to be substantially lower
than what we've seen the last three years, and that's going to drive the salmon price higher.
In addition, the COVID-19 pandemic has basically caused a lot of increase in the retail consumption,
which means that we have a lot of new retail consumers.
And it's my belief that once the food service industry as well normalises in 2021,
we're going to have a very solid demand response.
And the combination of solid demand and lower supply should mean a very favourable development for the salmon price.
And obviously also for the salmon farming companies.
So the backdrop is definitely interesting and we're looking forward to get Ivan's take on what's happening right now.
And we'll do this the way that you Alexander and I will take turns asking questions to Ivan.
And I'll kick off.
So first, Ivan, what would you say has been the biggest impact of COVID-19 on your business?
So far, by far, the salmon prices 40-45% of salmon is under normal circumstances consumed in food service.
And although we have seen increased demand in retail, it hasn't offset all of the loss in food service.
So in total, we think total demand is down by 10%.
Having said that, it is important to know that this is a short term character and purely connected to COVID-19 disruptions.
The underlying demand for salmon has and still is very strong.
Yeah, Ivan, just a follow-up question from my side there.
I mean, the demand side, that's one thing.
But what about the logistical challenges?
Particularly in the beginning, there was a lot of challenges with air freight.
Is that starting to normalise, or is that still a challenge for you?
Well, it's normalising.
I think it's the right way to phrase it.
Cost is down.
We also find capacity.
So in the end of the day now, it's about economics really.
So we think this development will continue.
And in opposed to COVID-19 situation, we think it will recover quite rapidly.
So you know how it is, to begin with, there is a lot of chaos.
And then you find ways around it and things gradually normalise.
So you think the second wave of COVID-19 we're seeing in some countries,
the effect is probably going to be less profound than during the first round?
Yeah, so far that is our take.
So we do not have any big problems with the logistic anymore
as we had to begin with.
It was actually really hard to get the volumes going.
So that has changed.
So it looks like the world is one way or another adapting to this virus
until we hopefully have a vaccine in place in due course.
And in terms of the net effect, I think you basically answered that question.
Retail obviously significantly up, food service significantly down.
And I think you mentioned roughly 10% impact.
So was that correct?
Yes, that's correct.
So far, but we also think this will gradually improve a vaccine or no vaccine
because we are building demand in retail.
Like for like, if you give us enough time, we think we will offset all the loss in food service
through the retail segment.
But our take is that we will have a post COVID-19 situation next year
and the food service will recover and at the same time retail is stronger than ever.
So the demand side of this looks favorable in our view
and you should shortly supply Alexander to begin with and next year looks constrained.
So we also have a very positive view on the market next year.
Of course, if COVID-19 for some reason doesn't improve or actually deteriorate
that could of course change this equation.
But that is not our scenario right now.
Ivan, I think in connection with the Q2 presentation
you pointed out that you're seeing a shift in demand towards more elaborated products.
Do you expect this shift to be permanent?
Over time, we think this development will continue.
I mean, we have seen this development over many years already.
Of course, it has increased in pace during the COVID-19 situation.
And the day after vaccine, you may see some changes.
But the long-term development will go on.
People will eat more and more elaborated salmon products.
That is absolutely our take on this.
So we have seen in the past, we have had low sun prices
that it has really boosted demand down the road.
This, we think, will happen again, particularly in the salmon category in retail.
And this takes us further on to your branding strategy.
Can you give us an update on how it is progressing?
I can.
So our more branding strategy is a long-term strategy
that continues unabated, notwithstanding some short-term
COVID-19 disruptions.
The strategy is closely connected to Movis' unique value chain
through an attractive name, good story, and products standing out.
Our ultimate goal is to decommodiatise the salmon category.
So nothing has changed.
But of course, we have had some short-term setbacks that goes without saying.
But again, this is a long-term strategy, which will continue unabated.
Yeah, in terms of the branding strategy,
obviously more salmon now being consumed in retail.
Do you think it's actually easier to get acceptance for branding in retail
versus food service?
Absolutely, from a long-term perspective.
This is strange enough good for our branding strategy.
Good to hear.
So turning to the supply and demand balance, Ivan.
How do you see it developing forward?
Well, we have already touched based upon it.
It looks favorable, constrained supply next year.
We think the next year is to come.
You will not see any meaningful volumes from land-based farming yet.
We also believe in a continued strong demand for our salmon.
So the only thing that could deteriorate this is COVID-19.
But notwithstanding COVID-19, we build markets as we speak.
So it just means that it will take some longer time.
But the prospect for this industry hasn't changed in our view.
It looks very favorable going forward.
I think that's a view I sort of share, particularly with regards to next year.
It's Chile in particular, which is driving the slow growth.
We're expecting Chile to decline due to lower release of salmon over the past 12 months.
So I would say I share that view.
Rising cost, I guess, is a theme that is hard to escape.
I would say in any sector or any business.
And to fight cost pressure, the board of movie have decided to include a productivity program in the cost-saving program,
targeting a 10% reduction in headcount for movie assets by 2024.
How does a 10% reduction in headcount match the growth potential of your business?
Good question.
Can we have two thoughts at once?
We think, yes, increasing productivity and growing at the same time are not mutually exclusive in our view.
We can do both.
So we absolutely believe this is feasible.
We and the industry have had a concerning development in cost the last decade, I would say.
So this we must address even harder going forward.
I think over the past few years we've obviously seen significant cost inflation due to higher price of fee.
That's one thing, but also the biological challenges.
It seems to me that at least on a sector level the production cost has at least stopped increasing lately.
And we're seeing good arguments for lower costs going forward, hopefully slightly higher volumes.
And also slightly better biological control, but could you probably give a little bit more flavor on the progress in terms of biology?
Are we starting to find better ways of living with sea lice or treating for sea lice?
And should I have a positive effect on cost going forward?
So to start with the beginning here, you said this is driven by biology and that's absolutely true.
And that gives us hope because biology you can change.
And we saw in the second quarter we had 35 Norwegian kroner per kilogram in cost in the ferros.
We were below 38 in region north. We were just below 40 in mid.
And then our highest cost performer had 58 nock.
So it shows that it's possible to deliver a good cost with the current environment.
So there's a lot of potential out there.
The challenge for more with this broad portfolio is that it's much easier to have things going wrong.
But that we have an untapped potential, absolutely.
And we are improving a little bit every day.
At the same time we are up against cost inflation like the others.
We also see strict regulations, et cetera.
But it's just to look to the best cost performers.
It's still doable to deliver good on cost.
I think it's almost like golf.
The best guys is the ones who do the least mistakes.
And we're seeing this positive science in certain regions.
And we're looking forward to the high cost regions starting to perform in line with your better regions as well.
That will be on my wish list.
Mine as well.
Good to hear.
There's a lot of new activity going on.
Ivan, you mentioned land based.
We see new farming regions, new technologies are emerging.
Hva vil du si i filmet?
Nå har vi vært og sett en strategi.
Det har vært mye gammel.
Det gir opptidigheter.
Vi monitorer det nødvendig.
Det er om time.
Hvor lang tid vi fikk før å åpne penning,
ble det en bra industri.
Mange, mange år.
Dette development må kanskje gå fast.
Men ingen vet det.
Det er interessant.
Det er veldig fint.
Det gir absolutt mange oppdater.
Hvis du åpner og gjør det stort.
Du sa at det er forskjellige teknologiske og direksjoner.
Er det en spesial teknologi som har kjent et ekstra atensjon?
Vi kan ikke være så spesifika.
Jeg vil si at det gir stort.
Nå vet ingen.
Vi ser det.
Men det kommer noen positive effekter,
eller nye teknologiske,
som vi kan skale opp.
Hvis vi starter på teknologiske side.
Det ser ut som de to mæne nye teknologiske farmer
som ser ut at det er land- og off-sjord.
I de fallet av projektene,
de er mest uten de to mæne kampene.
Er det en delig brutt strategi,
eller er det bare å vente og se hvordan det gir stort.
Ja, jeg tror du må lukke på den siste parten du sa.
Når vi må lukke opp hans mindre,
og hvordan vi gjør det,
vil vi lukke deg å kjente.
Men vi er at vi monitorer den nødvendig,
så vi har en høyt- og se strategi.
Hvis du sa at det gir stort.
Du er ikke sikker om å lukke en først begynne
i teknologiske side?
Nei, ikke virkelig.
I denne industrien har det ikke vært noe først begynne.
Det har vært det første begynne,
men selvfølgelig kan det ikke vært det første begynne.
Vi tror at det er mer om det største timning.
Timning er en nykkel her.
Hvis du tar landbasen, er det rasne teknologi.
Rasne teknologi er en ganske teknologi.
Vi er ras i små produksjoner.
Vi har gjort det for mange år.
Vi har en masse kompetens innhånd,
men vi har ikke tatt sammen med denne siden.
Så det er stille noe untørt grunn for oss.
Hvis du taler om landbasen,
har vi ikke sett en riktig successstoriet.
Så vi ser at det er stille ut.
Offshore farming, det er veldig ganske.
Hvis du taler om offshore farming,
så tar du også reglanser i stedet.
Så vi ser hvordan reglanser med det.
Så mange ting går på.
Det vi decide å gjøre,
er stil rett til å være annonset.
Det er omkring tidligere i dag.
Ivan, vil du si om vi finner mer avdømninger
til å develope en oppstrømning
enn enn en videnning i landbasen?
Nei, først,
jeg tror ikke at de er muttulig eksklusiv.
Jeg tror du kan gjøre alle det,
men vennstilene, vennstil fokuset,
det er vennstil. Det er vennstil.
Det er vår første prioritet.
Men jeg tror ikke at dette er muttulig eksklusiv.
Vi er fullt integreret.
Vi har det hele valget.
Vi benefitt ut av det,
vennstil i dag,
med COVID-19.
Vi har tatt på vennstil i dag.
Det er som om det er høy på investeringsagendaen.
Når det har vært en vennstil,
har du decide å kanske vennstil for Q1 og Q2.
Jeg vet at Alexander har tatt på å resumere vennstil
fra Q3.
Kan du ha noen koll på hva du kommunikerte
til investerere during Q2-reporten?
Jeg vet at jeg må i hvert år
ha tatt på vennstil.
Jeg kan gifte min koll på det.
Moe har en av de mest vennstilige vennstil.
Det er vennstil.
Hvis du genererer kass,
og du er nødvendig for denne operasjonen,
så blir det vennstil.
Og du har en nødvendig nødvendig target.
Det er også viktig å remember
at, for Moe spiller kvorklige,
hver andre sammenholder de vennstilige,
vi taler om de 2019 vennstilige,
Moe har spillet ut de enten 2019 vennstilige.
Vi taler om de 2020 vennstilige,
som de fleste andre vennstilige har ikke startet å diskutere.
Men i denne siden,
vi tror at de fortsatt spiller vennstilige
i en løde løde,
er at vi expecter denne markeringen
til å improve,
og ørningene til å improve.
Og hvis de Q3-nummerne er ute,
er vi ganske færdig ganske ganske...
Vi kaller det en refleksjonpunkt,
hvor companyene også kan si at,
ok, Covid-19 er startet til å bage,
vi ser at demand- og supplybalanser
bygger stort.
Og i denne siden blir vi mer komfortet
for å resumere vennstilige.
Hvis det er vennstilige.
Jeg forstår ikke hans relleksjon
på behalf av bordet.
Ja, selvfølgelig,
og en færdig point fra du, Alex.
Ok, så,
for å samle her,
i det enden,
hvis vi vil si at investere
skal begynne å begynne å begynne
å begynne å begynne å begynne
å begynne å begynne
å begynne å begynne
Covid-19-markedisruptjene.
Men at de lille fondementale
ble solid,
med potensiellt stort markedbalans
næste år, er det det?
Eva?
Det er det på stort.
Så vi tror
i en stort supply- og demand-
balans næste år,
har vi forstået
en post- Covid-19-situasjon.
Men
også i en Covid-19-situasjon,
vi ser at markedet
er gradually improving.
Vi ser at vi får
nye kunder, nye klienter.
Vi ser at vi bygger dem på.
Så vi får
eventuelt
med eller videre Covid-19.
Men selvfølgelig,
hvis vi er stille i en Covid-19-situasjon,
det blir en lang tid.
Så vi,
Samen har hatt en fantastisk
løsning,
og
vi tror stort
i en fantastisk fulga.
Det er en fantastisk produkt.
Takk så mye,
Johan.
Vi ser at vi får å follow en film,
selvfølgelig.
Takk også,
Alexander.
Takk til alle for å lese.
Takk for at jeg er Marius.
Takk, Alexander.
Takk for at jeg er Marius.
Innollige sendingen er ikke gjennomgått
eller godkjent av
D&B Markets avdelning for analyser.
Alle uttalser reflekterer
D&Bs vurderinger på tidspunktet
for den aktuelle sendingen,
og kan etterkant endres utnærmere beskjed.
D&B ikke er ansvarlig
for hvordan informasjonen i podcasten
benyttes eller tolkes.
Informasjonen som hennviser
til historisk avkastning i finansellinstrumenter,
i finansell indeks,
eller prognoser,
er en tid for fremtidig avkastning.
D&B gjør oppmerksom på at
en hver investering i et finansellest instrument
er forbundet med risiko for økonomisk tap.
Finansielle instrumenter
kan både øke og minnske valg,
slik at avkastning kan bli negativ
som følge av kursfall.
D&B tar inntil et ansvar
for direkte eller indirekte tap
og kostnader som måtte oppståve
bruk av den informasjonen som gis i podcasten.
